taxonomy.scoring_calibration: Confidence weighting or scoring logic required adjustment
taxonomy.evidence_reinterpretation: Evidence was weighted or classified incorrectly
taxonomy.drift_detection: Change detection model missed or misidentified a transition
taxonomy.resolver_trust: Upstream resolver behavior produced misleading results
taxonomy.false_positive: A finding was asserted that did not reflect reality

events.title: DMARC confidence weighting adjusted for aggregate-only policies
events.confidence_impact: ICIE weight recalibration: p=none+rua now scores higher than p=none without reporting. Prior model treated both identically, understating domains with partial monitoring.
events.resolution: Recalibrated ICIE weights to distinguish p=none with rua from p=none without reporting. Affected scans retroactively flagged in drift history.
events.bayesian_note: Prior P(secure|p=none) was uniform. Updated posterior splits on presence of rua= directive as differentiating evidence.

events.title: DANE TLSA timeout incorrectly scored as misconfiguration
events.confidence_impact: Verdict logic conflated absence-of-response with absence-of-record. Confidence was asserted at levels the evidence did not support.
events.resolution: Timeout now yields UNKNOWN status with degraded confidence instead of FAIL. Confidence engine marks result as low-certainty when resolver timeout occurs.
events.bayesian_note: P(misconfigured|timeout) was treated as ~1.0. Corrected to reflect that timeout is ambiguous evidence: P(misconfigured|timeout) ≈ P(network_issue|timeout).

events.title: Hash comparison missed CNAME-flattened record changes
events.confidence_impact: Drift engine false-negative rate was non-zero for CNAME-dependent records. Domains with CNAME flattening could change posture without triggering detection.
events.resolution: Drift engine now resolves CNAME chains before hashing canonical form. All historical drift baselines regenerated.
events.bayesian_note: Detection model assumed record stability at query level. Updated to normalize at canonical resolution level before computing hash.
